title: Kyoko and Langley split from Eva and personality disorders
display_title: Kyoko and Langley split from Eva and personality disorders
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jan 20, 2009 5:00 pm Well she was knocked up but then abandoned by a member of the US military... was the US less than forthright in reconstruction efforts after the war, perhaps? Japan developed fantastic auto and electronics industries for instance, which the US launched a smear campaign against in the 80's when they became a threat to US domestic businesses.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Jan 20, 2009 5:18 pm NemZ wrote: Well she was knocked up but then abandoned by a member of the US military...

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Jan 21, 2009 9:40 am BobBQ wrote: ...Who?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 21, 2009 12:33 pm The knocking and abandoning happened a few years apart, but it's still essentially true and cuts right down to the heart of the matter. ...and yes, this tangent has nothing to do with topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 21, 2009 3:49 pm Um, Kyoko wasn't knocked up by an American solider; Langley simply remarried after Kyoko's death. (And happened to have an affair with the doctor/Asuka's future stepmother when Kyoko went insane... )

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Wed Jan 21, 2009 3:53 pm NemZ wrote: Well she was knocked up but then abandoned by a member of the US military

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 21, 2009 4:11 pm First off, this is actually part of the larger tangent about eva as post-WW2 symbolism which has nothing to do with the original topic. Second, there is no other guy: Langley is who I'm talking about. I suppose I went with too confusing a choice of words but it's how Kyoko saw it: he was abandoning both her and Asuka, hence the 'die with me' stuff. Well, that plus crazy. Third, apologies if I was wrong in referring to Langley as a military man. I understood the situation as his being stationed in Germany when he met Kyoko, but I can't recall now where I got this notion from... probably some random fanfic if I had to guess. Oh well, my bad.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Wed Jan 21, 2009 4:17 pm NemZ wrote: but it's how Kyoko saw it: he was abandoning both her and Asuka, hence the 'die with me' stuff. Well, that plus crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 21, 2009 4:44 pm How am I getting it backwards? Contact experiment > langley fooling around with her doctor > die with me Asuka was abandoned by two mothers in a row and a father who apparently didn't pay her much attention. She threw herself into NERV because she perceived that it was all she had left. How her family sees it is another matter entirely, though from comments by stepmother I think she would agree with you. To the question of whom abandoned whom, my answer would be: yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Wed Jan 21, 2009 5:46 pm NemZ wrote: How am I getting it backwards?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 21, 2009 6:45 pm I think we are in violent agreement.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Wed Jan 21, 2009 6:47 pm Perish the thought
summary:

Japan developed fantastic auto and electronics industries for instance, which the US launched a smear campaign against in the 80's when they became a threat to US domestic businesses.
